pi_items.source: Task description (lines 5 of task.json and line 17 of metadata) specifies the code to be created. Code format verified as plausible custom code. Not a standard CPT code.

Task description (line 5) explicitly states 'Add a new Service/CPT code with... Description: Sports Physical Exam'. Verifier checks description contains 'Sports Physical' (case-insensitive). Sports Physical is a legitimate medical service per web search results on preventive care codes.

Task description (line 5) specifies 'Fee/Charge: $50.00'. Verifier checks fee equals 50.0. Web search confirms $50 is reasonable for preventive service codes (CPT 99395-99396 range $50-200+). Export script queries charge column and verifies exact match.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires adding a custom billing code to NOSH ChartingSystem (real open-source EHR system). Metadata claims specify target_code='SPT-PHY' (custom code, not standard CPT), target_description='Sports Physical Exam' (legitimate medical service), and target_fee=50.00 (reasonable fee). All three metadata values exactly match the explicit task specifications in task.json description field. The code 'SPT-PHY' is synthetic/fabricated for this task (not a standard medical code like numeric CPT codes 99395-99396). Database schema verified: NOSH uses cpt table with columns: code, code_text (description), charge (fee), active status, consistent with OpenEMR structure. Data is synthetic in the sense that the custom code is created by the agent during task execution, not retrieved from existing data sources.
